plays.name: Inside Zone
plays.desc: Downhill zone between the tackles. Two combo double-teams drive the front and climb to the linebackers. RB aims the playside A-gap and makes one cut.
plays.note: Covered/uncovered rule: defender over you, block him; nobody over you, help double-team then climb to a linebacker. vs a 4-3 (even) front the combos shift: C+RG double the 3-tech up to MIKE, LG bases the nose shade, tackles base the ends.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Install this first. The two COMBO double-teams ARE the play — drill them until they're automatic before adding anything else.
rows: Two double-teams on the down linemen, climbing to the ILBs (vs 3-4: RT+RG the end, C+LG the Nose · vs 4-3: C+RG the 3-tech, LG bases the shade).
Take the handoff, aim playside A-gap. One cut, get downhill.
Hand it off, carry out a fake.

plays.name: Outside Zone
plays.desc: Wide/stretch zone. Every lineman takes a flat reach step playside, sealing defenders inside. RB presses the edge and bounces or cuts up.
plays.note: Same covered/uncovered zone rule as inside zone — only the footwork (flat reach, never step backside) and the RB aim point (the edge) change.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Pairs with inside zone off the same rules. Teach the flat reach step and "one cut, get downhill."
rows: Flat reach step playside — arc to your man's playside shoulder, run him to the sideline or hook him.
Reach step, then climb flat to the nearest playside linebacker.
Reach the playside end — he sets the RB's read.
Press the outside hip of the tackle. End reached = bounce, end hooked = one cut upfield.
Open playside, hand it off deep, carry out the boot fake.

plays.name: Inside Trap
plays.desc: Backside guard pulls and kicks out the first defender past the center; everyone else blocks down. Quick downhill hitter — the trapped man blocks himself by crashing upfield.
plays.note: The trap man (marked) is left free on purpose — his upfield crash is what lets the puller kick him. vs a 3-4 that's the playside end, and the Nose gets combo'd (C+RG) up to the backside ILB — you never trap a nose head-up on the center. vs a 4-3 the trap man is the 3-tech, with the center back-blocking the shade.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: One puller, downhill, simple aim point. The RB reads the pulling guard's hip — duck inside the kick-out, never bounce around it.
rows: PULL & TRAP the first man past the C (end vs 3-4, 3-tech vs 4-3) — hit his inside hip.
vs 3-4: COMBO the Nose up to the backside ILB. vs 4-3: C back-blocks the shade, RG influences and climbs to MIKE.
vs 3-4: climb to MIKE. vs 4-3: base the end. Never touch the trap man.
Take the handoff downhill right off the trap. Be patient, duck inside the kick.

plays.name: Sweep Strong
plays.desc: Toss/sweep to the strong side; backside guard and Y (FB) pull/lead around the edge while playside seals inside.
plays.note: Sweep gets your best athlete on the perimeter behind a wall of pullers.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Drill the toss exchange clean before adding speed — a bobbled toss is a big loss waiting to happen.
rows: Down block inside (Nose vs 3-4, 3-tech vs 4-3) — nobody crosses your face.
PULL playside, lead around the edge on the force defender.
Lead block — wrap the edge, kick the first man outside.
Take the toss, follow the pullers around the edge, get vertical.

plays.name: H Rocket Sweep
plays.desc: H comes in fast jet motion and takes the toss on the move; QB reverse-pivots. Speed sweep — the motion does the work.
plays.note: Jet/rocket sweep lives on the motion man's speed. The line just reach-seals.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The whole play is snap timing — H should be at full speed as he crosses the QB. Rep the timing until it's automatic.
rows: Reach playside (zone) — seal everyone inside, run them to the sideline.
Jet motion full speed, take the toss on the move, get vertical off the edge.
Reverse-pivot, hand or toss to H in motion, carry out a fake.

plays.name: QB Trap Strong
plays.desc: Inside trap but the QB keeps it; a back leads through the hole. Same trap blocking as Inside Trap.
plays.note: QB-run version of trap. Use it when your QB is one of your better runners.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Call it when your QB is one of your better runners. No exchange to mess up — keep and follow the lead back.
rows: PULL & TRAP the first man past the C (end vs 3-4, 3-tech vs 4-3).
vs 3-4: COMBO the Nose up to the backside ILB. vs 4-3: C back-blocks the shade, RG climbs to MIKE.
vs 3-4: climb to MIKE. vs 4-3: base the end. Never touch the trap man.
Lead through the hole, block the first wrong color.
Keep the ball, follow the lead back downhill off the trap.

plays.name: Inside Zone Read
plays.desc: Inside zone with the QB reading the backside edge. Give if he crashes, keep if he sits.
plays.note: The unblocked highlighted defender is the read. One key, give-or-keep.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Install the CALLED version first — a straight inside-zone handoff. Turn the read live only once the zone blocking is solid.
rows: Inside-zone rules (vs 3-4: RG+RT the end, C+LG the Nose · vs 4-3: C+RG the 3-tech, LG the shade).
LEAVE the backside read man — block down inside.
Run inside zone, aim playside A. Take the give unless QB pulls.
READ the backside edge: crashes → pull & keep. Sits → give to RB.

plays.name: Toss Crack
plays.desc: Toss to the strong side; the slot/wing cracks down on the force defender while the outside receiver blocks the corner.
plays.note: The crack block by the slot springs the edge — he blocks the force man down so the runner gets outside.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The crack block must be above the waist and from the front — coach it hard, it's the block that springs the play.
rows: Pull and lead around the edge.
CRACK down on the force defender (S) from outside-in.
Take the toss, follow the crack and pull, turn it up.

plays.name: Z Around
plays.desc: Fake jet to H one way, then Z comes around the other way for the ball. Misdirection reverse — high risk/reward.
plays.note: Misdirection off your jet look — the fake holds the defense while Z attacks the other side.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Hardest ball-handling in the set (two motions, fake + real exchange). Save it as a once-a-game shot play.
rows: Reach/seal away from the play to sell the jet direction.
Fake jet sweep one way — sell it, no ball.
Come around behind for the real handoff, attack the opposite edge.
Fake to H, give to Z coming around.

plays.name: Reverse Option
plays.desc: Jet fake, QB keeps and runs the reverse-side edge, optioning to Y as the pitch man. A reverse AND an option.
plays.note: A reverse fake into a true edge option. Two decisions for the QB.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Start with the CALLED keep-only version — a reverse fake plus a live pitch is the toughest ball-handling in the book.
rows: Fake jet the other way — sell it.
READ the force defender (S): takes you → pitch to Y. Sits → keep upfield.

plays.name: Toss Bob
plays.desc: Toss with a back-on-backer (Bob) lead. The backside tackle pulls and leads up on the backside linebacker.
plays.note: "Bob" = back-on-backer; a lead-blocking toss. Simple and physical.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Run it as a straight call — simple, physical, downhill.
rows: Block the man over/inside you (end vs 3-4, 3-tech vs 4-3).
Block the Nose (vs 4-3: climb to WILL).
Climb to the backside ILB (vs 4-3: base the shade).
Pull and lead (Bob) up on MIKE through the hole.
Lead through the hole (vs 4-3: kick out the playside end).
Take the toss, follow Y and the puller.
Toss to RB, carry out the fake.

plays.name: FB Belly Weak
plays.desc: Inside give to the weak side with belly action; Y (FB) and Z misdirect the other way. Clean downhill run.
plays.note: Belly is a downhill inside give with misdirection window dressing to hold the backside.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Great early-down call. Belly hits the WEAK side by design — opposite most of the run game.
rows: Take the give, hit it weak side downhill.
Give to Y, fake the other way.

plays.name: Jet Sweep
plays.desc: The motion man flies across at full speed and takes the handoff on the move, hitting the edge. The pre-snap speed stresses the defense — pure perimeter run.
plays.note: Jet sweep lives on the motion man's speed. The line just reach-seals and the back beats everyone to the corner.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The whole play is timing — snap the ball as the motion man crosses the QB at full speed.
rows: Reach playside (zone) — seal everyone inside, run them to the sideline.
Lead or seal the edge force defender.
Full-speed jet motion, take the handoff on the move, get vertical off the edge.
Catch the snap, hand to the jet man crossing, carry out a fake.

plays.name: Jet + Inside Zone
plays.desc: Fake the jet sweep to the motion man, hand inside zone to the RB the other way. The jet motion pulls a defender to the edge, opening the inside run.
plays.note: The jet fake pulls the edge defender (and often a linebacker) toward the sideline — that's what opens the inside zone.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Call it once the defense starts chasing your jet sweep — the inside give will be wide open.
rows: Inside-zone rules (vs 3-4: RT+RG the end, C+LG the Nose · vs 4-3: C+RG the 3-tech, LG the shade).
FAKE the jet sweep — sell it at full speed, no ball.
Take the inside-zone give, aim playside A-gap, one cut.
Fake the jet, hand inside to the RB.

plays.name: Orbit Toss
plays.desc: The motion man loops (orbits) behind the QB, then the QB tosses to the RB going the other way with the orbit man leading or as a second threat.
plays.note: The orbit motion behind the QB freezes backside defenders and gives a second edge threat off the toss.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Good change-up off the toss look. The orbit loop is slower than jet, so the timing is more forgiving.
rows: Pull and lead around the edge.
Orbit behind the QB — second threat / lead, holds the backside.
Take the toss, follow the pullers around the edge.
Toss to the RB, the orbit man loops as a decoy/lead.

plays.name: Slant-Flat
plays.desc: Mirrored quick game: outside receivers run slants, slots shoot to the flats. QB takes three steps and the ball is out — a two-man hi-lo on the flat defender on both sides.
plays.note: Read the flat defender on your chosen side: he widens with the flat → throw the slant behind him; he sits → throw the flat now.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Best first pass install. Three steps, pick a side pre-snap, ball is OUT — quick game means no sacks.
rows: Pass set — quick game, wall up inside, nobody touches the QB in 3 steps.
Slant: 3 hard steps upfield, snap it inside across the defender's face.
Shoot to the flat at 2 yards, eyes back for the ball immediately.
Scan protection inside-out, help where the pressure comes.
3-step drop. Pick a side pre-snap. Flat defender widens = slant, sits = flat.

plays.name: Stick
plays.desc: Trips quick game. The outside receiver clears deep, the middle slot sticks at 5 yards, and the inside slot shoots the flat — a hi-lo triangle on the flat defender with a backside slant answer.
plays.note: Read the flat defender: he runs to the flat → throw the stick behind him; he hangs on the stick → throw the flat now. Backside slant vs a soft corner.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The stick runner settles in the first open window past the line — sit down vs zone, keep rolling away vs man.
rows: Pass set — ball is out in 3 steps.
Outside vertical — clear the corner out of the picture.
STICK at 5: push upfield, snap out and settle in the first window.
Fast flat at 2 yards — stretch the flat defender wide.
Backside slant — the answer if the defense floods to trips.
3-step drop. Flat defender takes the flat = stick; hangs = flat. Late, work back to X.

plays.name: Smash
plays.desc: Classic corner hi-lo, mirrored: outside receivers run 6-yard hitches, slots run corner routes over the top. The playside corner cannot cover both.
plays.note: Read the playside corner (highlighted): he sinks under the corner route → throw the hitch; he squats on the hitch → throw the corner over his head.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The corner route breaks at 10 to the front pylon — teach the slot to bend it away from the safety, never flat to the sideline.
rows: Pass set — 5-step timing, hold the pocket.
Hitch at 6 — snap back to the QB, sit in front of the corner.
Corner route: stem to 10, break to the front pylon over the hitch.
Pass protect — check inside pressure first.
5-step drop, pick a side. Corner sinks = hitch now; corner sits = corner route over him.

plays.name: Curl-Flat
plays.desc: Mirrored curl-flat: outside receivers curl at 10, slots run to the flats, RB leaks as the checkdown. Another hi-lo stretch, one level deeper than quick game.
plays.note: Same read as slant-flat, one level deeper: flat defender widens → curl; hangs under the curl → flat. Nothing there, dump the checkdown.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Teach the curl runner to come back DOWNHILL to the ball — drifting curls get jumped and picked.
rows: Curl at 10: push hard vertical, snap back downhill to the QB.
Flat at 3 — stretch the flat defender away from the curl window.
Check protection, then leak to the flat as the checkdown.
5-step drop. Flat defender widens = curl; hangs = flat; nothing = checkdown to the RB.

plays.name: Four Verticals
plays.desc: Four receivers straight down the field splitting the deep coverage. Outside receivers take the numbers, slots run the seams. One-high safety can't cover both seams.
plays.note: vs 1-high: throw the seam away from the free safety. vs 2-high: the seams bend inside and the QB looks outside to the fade one-on-ones.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Spacing IS the play — four lanes, evenly spaced, nobody drifts together. Throw it when the safety commits, not before.
rows: Pass set — this one takes time, protect the full drop.
Outside verticals — stay on the numbers, win the one-on-one.
Seams — split the hashes, eyes for the ball at 12-15 yards.
5-step drop, read the deep safety: he picks a seam, throw the other one.

plays.name: Boot Flood
plays.desc: Play-action boot: fake the zone run left, QB rolls right behind a 3-level flood — vertical clearing the top, TE sail at 12, shallow drag underneath, with a backside dig trailing late.
plays.note: Three levels, one side of the field: deep (Z), middle (Y sail), short (H drag). QB reads high to low on the run. The run fake is what opens all of it.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The fake must LOOK like your zone run — same footwork, same pace. If the QB rushes the fake, nobody bites and the boot dies.
rows: Slide left with the run fake; playside stays firm — no one downfield.
Sell the zone fake left — great fakes get tackled.
Vertical — take the top off the playside.
Sail: release flat, bend to 12 yards toward the sideline. #1 target.
Shallow drag across, settle at 4-5 yards as the easy throw.
Backside dig at 12 — the late window if the QB pulls up.
Fake the zone, boot right. Read Z deep → Y sail → H drag. Run it if the edge is clean.

plays.name: Slot Screen
plays.desc: Quick tunnel screen to the slot: Y works back inside behind the outside receiver's block while the backside runs off the coverage. Free yards vs soft corners and heavy blitz teams.
plays.note: The throw is now — catch the snap, one step, out. Z's block on the corner is the whole play.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Throw it slightly behind the screen runner so he catches it coming downhill inside — never lead him to the sideline.
rows: Show pass set — sell dropback for one count.
Take one step up, then work back inside behind Z's block. Catch and get north.
Stalk the corner — this block IS the play.
Sprint verticals — run your defenders out of the picture.
Protect the QB's face for the one count it takes.
Catch, one step, throw now — slightly behind Y so he turns upfield.

plays.name: Spread Slant-Flat Lt
plays.desc: Quick-game hi-lo on the playside flat defender: slant behind him, flat in front of him.
plays.note: Flat defender widens with the flat → throw the slant behind his ear. He sits → throw the flat NOW. Flip direction in the viewer to run it the other way.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Three steps and the ball is OUT. Quick game means the line only has to win for a second.
rows: Pass set — quick game, wall up inside for one second.
Slant — 3 hard steps up, snap it inside across his face. Ball can come first window.
Speed flat at 2 — stretch the flat defender, eyes back NOW.
AUTOMATIC slant — backside answer. Blitz or soft corner = QB comes here now.
AUTOMATIC slant — backside answer. Blitz or soft corner = QB comes here now.
Seam — split the safeties, hold the middle open. (yours — only two receivers playside)
Flat defender widens with the flat → throw the slant behind his ear. Playside is LEFT — backside slants are your blitz answer.

plays.name: Spread Stick Lt
plays.desc: The outside receiver clears the corner deep; the stick settles at 5 in the first window; the flat stretches underneath — a triangle the flat defender cannot cover.
plays.note: Flat defender runs to the flat → stick. He hangs on the stick → flat. Never both wrong. Flip direction in the viewer to run it the other way.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The stick runner SITS vs zone, keeps sliding away vs man. Teach the difference.

Vertical — run the corner off, take the top off the coverage.

Flat defender runs to the flat → stick. Playside is LEFT — backside slants are your blitz answer.

plays.name: Spread Smash Lt

plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The corner route bends to the FRONT pylon, away from the safety — never flat to the sideline.

plays.name: Spread Curl-Flat Lt
plays.desc: Curl at 10 with a flat underneath — the same hi-lo as quick game, one level deeper.
plays.note: Flat defender widens → curl behind him. He hangs under the curl → flat. Nothing → checkdown. Flip direction in the viewer to run it the other way.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: Curls come back DOWNHILL to the ball. A drifting curl is an interception.

Curl at 10 — push hard vertical, snap back downhill to the QB.
Flat at 3 — widen the flat defender away from the curl window.

Flat defender widens → curl behind him. Playside is LEFT — backside slants are your blitz answer.

plays.name: Spread Flood Lt
plays.desc: Three levels on one sideline — deep, 12-yard sail, and flat — with the QB rolling to the flood. Somebody is always uncovered.
plays.note: Read high to low on the move: deep shot → sail → flat. Tuck and run if the edge is grass. Flip direction in the viewer to run it the other way.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The sail runner is the #1 target — flat release, then bend to 12 on the numbers.
rows: Pass set — full protection, give the QB the whole drop.
Vertical — clear the top of the flood side.
SAIL — release flat, bend to 12 toward the sideline. #1 in the progression.
AUTOMATIC slant — backside answer. Blitz or soft corner = QB comes here now.

Protect the rollout edge — nobody touches the QB’s back.
Read high to low on the move: deep shot → sail → flat. Playside is LEFT — backside slants are your blitz answer.

plays.name: Spread Mesh Lt
plays.desc: Two shallow crossers rub past each other in the middle — man coverage runs into traffic, zone gives up the sit. A corner route caps it.
plays.note: vs man: the crossers keep running away. vs zone: they SIT in the first hole past the mesh. Corner route is the shot. Flip direction in the viewer to run it the other way.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The mesh point is at 5 yards over the ball — bottom crosser sets it, top crosser brushes right over him.

plays.desc: Every receiver straight down the field, evenly spaced. One deep safety cannot cover two seams.
plays.note: One-high: throw the seam away from the safety. Two-high: seams bend inside, look to the outside one-on-ones. Flip direction in the viewer to run it the other way.

Seam — split the hash, eyes for the ball at 12-15.

plays.desc: The snag triangle: a settle route at 4 inside, a corner over the top, a flat underneath — high-low AND inside-out on two defenders at once.
plays.note: Corner defender takes the flat → snag sits open. He squeezes the snag → flat now. Shot = corner route vs a squatting corner. Flip direction in the viewer to run it the other way.

plays.desc: Two in-breaking routes at different depths — a dig at 12 over a shallow in at 5 — flooding the same inside zone window.

plays.desc: A shallow crosser sprints under the ball while a deep cross comes over the top from the other side — a two-level crossing flood that kills man coverage.
plays.note: Progression: shallow first (easy pitch-and-catch on the run), deep cross behind the backers, then the backside slant. Flip direction in the viewer to run it the other way.

plays.name: Wildcat Flood Rt
plays.desc: Three levels on one sideline — deep, 12-yard sail, and flat — with the QB rolling to the flood. Somebody is always uncovered. Wildcat version: the back is the passer off the direct snap.
plays.note: Read high to low on the move: deep shot → sail → flat. Tuck and run if the edge is grass. Flip direction in the viewer to run it the other way.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The sail runner is the #1 target — flat release, then bend to 12 on the numbers.
rows: Pass set — full protection, give the QB the whole drop.
Vertical — clear the top of the flood side.
SAIL — release flat, bend to 12 toward the sideline. #1 in the progression.

plays.name: Wildcat Mesh Rt
plays.desc: Two shallow crossers rub past each other in the middle — man coverage runs into traffic, zone gives up the sit. A corner route caps it. Wildcat version: the back is the passer off the direct snap.
plays.note: vs man: the crossers keep running away. vs zone: they SIT in the first hole past the mesh. Corner route is the shot. Flip direction in the viewer to run it the other way.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The mesh point is at 5 yards over the ball — bottom crosser sets it, top crosser brushes right over him.
rows: Pass set — full protection, give the QB the whole drop.
Corner — the shot over the mesh traffic.
LOW mesh — cross at 4, set the mesh point over the ball.
HIGH mesh — cross at 6, brush right over your mesh partner.

plays.name: Wildcat Four Verticals Rt
plays.desc: Every receiver straight down the field, evenly spaced. One deep safety cannot cover two seams. Wildcat version: the back is the passer off the direct snap.
plays.note: One-high: throw the seam away from the safety. Two-high: seams bend inside, look to the outside one-on-ones. Flip direction in the viewer to run it the other way.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: SPACING is the play — outside on the numbers, seams split the hashes. Nobody drifts together.
rows: Pass set — full protection, give the QB the whole drop.

plays.name: Wildcat Snag Rt
plays.desc: The snag triangle: a settle route at 4 inside, a corner over the top, a flat underneath — high-low AND inside-out on two defenders at once. Wildcat version: the back is the passer off the direct snap.
plays.note: Corner defender takes the flat → snag sits open. He squeezes the snag → flat now. Shot = corner route vs a squatting corner. Flip direction in the viewer to run it the other way.
plays.youthNote: Coaching point: The snag runner slants in for 3 steps then SITS at 4-5, inside the corner defender.
rows: Pass set — quick game, wall up inside for one second.
SNAG — slant inside for 3 steps, sit down at 4 in the soft spot.
Corner — over the top of the snag, break to the pylon.
Flat — sprint out low, stretch the coverage wide.
